summary:
Agent2 concludes based on specific stains' capabilities regarding their relevance to carbohydrates present in granular dystrophy; therefore, they reiterate support for using Periodic Acid-Schiff (PAS) as opposed to Masson Trichrome due to its superior ability to highlight critical components associated with this condition. To diagnose granular dystrophy effectively through histopathological examination, periodic acid-silver carbol fuchsin or similar methods highlighting mucopolysaccarides should ideally be employed rather than general trichromes like Masson’s because of the distinctive biochemical nature involved within granules characteristic of this disease state. **(Note:** Adjusted summary format per instructions while retaining core content.)** Summary:

To identify granular dystrophy in the cornea, Periodic Acid-Schiff (PAS) staining is preferred over Masson Trichrome because PAS highlights the glycogen-like material typically found in these deposits. This makes PAS more suitable for accurate diagnosis by visualizing crucial pathological features distinctly related to granular dystrophy. 

**(Condensed into two concise statements focusing only on clinically significant aspects)**
